titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Council of Masters, Yale University, Records
Council of Masters, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Mary Caldera

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 12, 2020 at 08:50
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Council of Masters, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records, generally maintained by the chair of the council, consist of correspondence, memoranda, reports, studies and surveys, committee files, and subject files documenting the policies and procedures of the Yale Council of Masters, which oversees affairs related to two or more residential colleges. The records do not include the Council of Masters meeting files, which are classed in Record Unit 299.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 13-A (YRG 13-A), Records of the Yale Council of Masters.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access is partially rectricted. See Collection Contents for details.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Council of Masters, Yale University, Records (RU 295). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Council of Masters, Yale University, Minutes of Meetings (RU 299), Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged in three series: I. Correspondence, 1934-1997. II. Subject Files, 1934-1992. III. Restricted files. and in subsequent accessions.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Yale University's residential colleges, modeled after those at Cambridge and Oxford, were conceived in the 1920s and designed to recapture the intimate collegiate atmosphere of an earlier time when class sizes small. The Council of Masters was established in 1932, the year the first residential colleges opened their doors. The first masters were appointed before the colleges were built, and as the Committee of Quadrangles, were instrumental in forming early policies regarding the colleges. The council consists of the masters, or administrative heads, of the residential colleges. Ex-officio members include the university president, provost, dean of Yale College, associate dean of Yale College and associate dean for student affairs. The Council makes recommendations to the university president in matters of policy concerning the residential colleges. It also serves as the committee of control in affairs concerning two or more residential colleges.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records, generally maintained by the chair of the council, consist of correspondence, memoranda, reports, studies and surveys, committee files, and subject files documenting the policies and procedures of the Yale Council of Masters, which oversees affairs related to two or more residential colleges. The records do not include the Council of Masters meeting files, which are classed in Record Unit 299.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the correspondence is restricted 35 years from the year the records were created.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of correspondence of the chair of the Council of Masters primarily with other members of the council, as well as, other administrative bodies at Yale. The correspondence documents general administrative functions and policies.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted. They will be available in 2019.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted. They will be available in 2019.

accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted. They will be available in 2023.

accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted. They will be available in 2025.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted until January 2027.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted until January 2028.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted until January 2029.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted until January 2030.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted. They will be available January 1, 2031.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted. They will be open January 1, 2033.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until Jan 1, 2028

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of records, listed alphabetically by subject, documenting the activities of the Council of Masters, including the records of various committees, filed by committee name.

did.unittitle: Budget and Fellowships, Committee on the
Creative and Performing Arts, Committee on
Faculty offices and Study of physical facilities
Gay [and lesbian] student counseling, proposal for
Language Laboratory - Facilities in the colleges
Legal Counseling - Draft and other matters
Mailings and meetings reminders, Checklist for

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also: series III, box 35, folder 17

did.unittitle: Teaching appointments - graduate students, faculty, college tutors
Teaching in the Residential Colleges, Committee on
Yale Science and Engineering Association (YSEA) Fellows: Graduate

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until Jan 1, 2059

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of records with personally identifiable information, which is restricted for 75 years from when the person left Yale or five years after death.

did.unittitle: Development - Library: Early lists of prospects

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
